text_segments.content: A dense section of hull surface, tiled at a microscopic scale. No mechanical parts. No networking substrate. But it faces the star.

A metabolically active tissue layer that converts ambient stellar radiation into glucose reserves. Requires approximately {required_power} power unit(s) to sustain activity. Each unit contributes {food_output_per_cycle} food per cycle.

Currently controlled: {controlled_count} unit(s). Total food contribution: {total_food_contribution} per cycle.
A coiled fluid-filled cavity, tucked against one of the larger processing clusters. Its geometry is almost architectural — precise curves, sealed membranes, no obvious input or output.

A fluid-filled sensory organ. Processes directional and spatial signal gradients, improving the clarity of information reaching connected nodes. Requires {required_power} power unit(s) to sustain. Each active unit contributes {signal_clarity_per_cycle} clarity per cycle.

Currently controlled: {controlled_count} unit(s).
A dense, pale deposit. No motion. It just sits there.

A dense metabolic buffer that stores glucose. No workers required—only connection to your controlled systems.

Current capacity: {current_glucose} / {max_glucose} glucose
Charge rate: {charge_rate}/cycle
Discharge rate: {discharge_rate}/cycle

Discharging food: {discharge_rate} units/cycle (≈ {proportional_food}% of total available)
